cot_prefix: Okay, let's take a look at this question. The article starts by saying that there are ten times as many Chinese newspapers and magazines now compared to 30 years ago. It gives specific numbers: 186 newspapers and 930 magazines in 1978 versus 2,081 newspapers and 9,363 magazines today. It also mentions that there are more publishing houses and books now. The article attributes this growth to the reform and opening-up policy, which led to rapid economic development and universal education. Also discussed is the restructuring of publishing houses and newspapers to adapt to a market economy, as well as legal changes in the industry.
